because.text: Accepted in full; all probes reproduced exactly (order flips phase 8192<->40960 and 0<->16384 on identical operands; decay chain; crystallized fixed point). Two blind reviewers converged on selection-only warrant-carried federation from independent attack paths - the strongest signal a design gate can produce. Beyond convergence: the fold is closed constructively (hash order grindable, (ph,hash) bakes phase priority into the sort key, ts forgeable per Warrant s5.1's own wall-clock rule, DAG order partial - safe aggregation must be commutative by construction, i.e. a new federation algebra, not interfere reuse); the personalized-term open question from gate 1 is CLOSED - binding a check to {node,wave,actor,epoch} proves per-subject replayability but one expensive proof still backs unlimited subjects; weight needs policy uniqueness domains and reuse caps (proof supports facts, policy meters weight); criterion 5 corrected (bounded state = cache property, authoritative state = settlement-active warrant DAG - fold-and-forget unsound against supersedes/key-state/late blobs); assertion blob schema sigma-glyph.wave-assertion@v1 and AnnotationViewID accepted as drafting bases; criterion 6 (auditability/projection) added. Kimi runs third with both reviews as priors: adjudicate F1-strict vs F1.5 extension point, attack ViewID and the zero-or-one-or-conflict-set selection rule.

subject.note: Codex ADR-006 gate review 2: F1.5 - blind convergence with Gemini on selection-only federation; fold closed constructively; ViewID + assertion schema contributed
